mapping.a_element: feasible range of excess demand (set of admissible market outcomes)
mapping.b_element: allowed region of 4-point Wilson coefficients (set of admissible EFTs)

mapping.a_element: constraints defining the feasible set (market-clearing conditions)
mapping.b_element: constraints defining the allowed set (factorization, peculiar parity, positivity)

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic notion of a constraint-defined region in a parameter space; the driving machinery differs. Paper A runs a penalty/Rockafellian relaxation whose norm-penalized slack converges as the penalty grows to a minimum-norm residual measuring distance to feasibility, whereas Paper B uses tree-level factorization plus positivity to carve out and bound an allowed coefficient region and identify its extremal 'corner' theories (Virasoro-Shapiro, infinite spin tower) - a bootstrap, not a penalty minimization, so no role-for-role mapping of the core mechanism holds.
